pattern: Work in time scaled by speed so every quantity is an integer, then run a DP over roads where the state is the number of skips used and the value is the minimum scaled arrival time, rounding up between roads only when no skip is taken.
hints: Multiply all times by speed so distances become the scaled travel cost and the integer-hour rest becomes rounding up to the next multiple of speed.
Let dp[j] be the least scaled arrival time after the current road using exactly j skips; from each state you either rest (round up, no skip) or skip (no rounding, one more skip).
After processing all roads, return the smallest j whose scaled time is at most hoursBefore times speed, or -1 if none qualifies.

edge_inputs.description: single road, no rest ever required
single road too slow to make it

stress.description: random short road lists with feasible deadlines
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 60)
    dist = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    speed = rng.randint(1, 50)
    # generous deadline so many cases are feasible
    hours = rng.randint(1, 5000)
    return [dist, speed, hours]

stress.description: tight deadlines to exercise the skip count search
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 40)
    dist = [rng.randint(1, 200) for _ in range(n)]
    speed = rng.randint(1, 30)
    base = sum((d + speed - 1) // speed for d in dist)
    hours = max(1, base - rng.randint(0, n))
    return [dist, speed, hours]
